cases.passage: The water softener system in a house uses the principle of equilibrium to remove calcium and magnesium ions from hard water. The system contains a resin that exchanges sodium ions for calcium and magnesium ions. If 25 grams of calcium carbonate is added to 500 grams of water, will the equilibrium be shifted in favor of the forward reaction or the reverse reaction? Assume the equilibrium constant (Kc) for the reaction is 1.5.

questions.q: What type of equilibrium is described in the passage?
opts: Dynamic equilibrium
Chemical equilibrium
Phase equilibrium
Equilibrium involving gases
questions.explanation: The passage describes a system where the forward and reverse reactions occur at the same rate, which is a characteristic of dynamic equilibrium.

questions.q: What is the effect of adding calcium carbonate on the equilibrium?
opts: The equilibrium will shift in favor of the forward reaction
The equilibrium will shift in favor of the reverse reaction
The equilibrium will not shift
The equilibrium will be disturbed temporarily
questions.explanation: Adding calcium carbonate will cause the equilibrium to shift in favor of the forward reaction, as it increases the concentration of reactants.

questions.q: What is the significance of the equilibrium constant (Kc) in this reaction?
opts: It determines the direction of the equilibrium
It determines the rate of the forward reaction
It determines the rate of the reverse reaction
It determines the extent of the reaction
questions.explanation: The equilibrium constant (Kc) determines the extent of the reaction, which in this case is 1.5.

questions.q: What type of ions are exchanged between the resin and the water in the water softener system?
opts: Sodium and calcium ions
Sodium and magnesium ions
Calcium and magnesium ions
Potassium and sodium ions
questions.explanation: The passage states that sodium ions are exchanged for calcium and magnesium ions in the water softener system.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the water softener system in the house?
opts: To remove impurities from water
To add minerals to water
To remove calcium and magnesium ions from hard water
To increase the pH of water
questions.explanation: The passage states that the water softener system uses the principle of equilibrium to remove calcium and magnesium ions from hard water.

cases.passage: A mixture of nitrogen and hydrogen gases is placed in a container and allowed to react to form ammonia gas. The reaction is as follows: N2(g) + 3H2(g) ⇌ 2NH3(g). If the initial concentration of nitrogen gas is 1.0 M and the initial concentration of hydrogen gas is 2.0 M, what will be the equilibrium concentration of ammonia gas if the equilibrium constant (Kc) for the reaction is 3.5?

questions.q: What is the direction of the reaction?
opts: Forward reaction
Reverse reaction
No change in the reaction
Depends on the initial concentrations of reactants
questions.explanation: The passage states that the reaction is allowed to proceed, which implies that the forward reaction is occurring.

questions.q: What is the effect of increasing the concentration of nitrogen gas on the equilibrium?
opts: The equilibrium will shift in favor of the forward reaction
The equilibrium will shift in favor of the reverse reaction
The equilibrium will not shift
The equilibrium will be disturbed temporarily
questions.explanation: Increasing the concentration of nitrogen gas will cause the equilibrium to shift in favor of the reverse reaction, as it decreases the concentration of reactants.

questions.q: What is the relationship between the equilibrium constant (Kc) and the equilibrium concentration of ammonia gas?
opts: Kc is directly proportional to the equilibrium concentration of ammonia gas
Kc is inversely proportional to the equilibrium concentration of ammonia gas
Kc is independent of the equilibrium concentration of ammonia gas
Kc is a measure of the equilibrium concentration of ammonia gas
questions.explanation: The equilibrium constant (Kc) is a measure of the ratio of the equilibrium concentrations of products to reactants, which in this case is 3.5.

questions.q: What is the effect of decreasing the concentration of hydrogen gas on the equilibrium?
opts: The equilibrium will shift in favor of the forward reaction
The equilibrium will shift in favor of the reverse reaction
The equilibrium will not shift
The equilibrium will be disturbed temporarily
questions.explanation: Decreasing the concentration of hydrogen gas will cause the equilibrium to shift in favor of the forward reaction, as it increases the concentration of reactants.

questions.q: What is the initial concentration of hydrogen gas in the mixture?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the initial concentration of hydrogen gas is 2.0 M.
